title: Calling Them “Boys”: A Short History of Words That Age Badly
summary: There is a particular kind of Zambian political mishap that does not require a scandal, a leaked memo, or a court case. It only requires a microphone, a moment of irritation, and one word too many. This week’s lesson in the genre comes courtesy of the word “boys”, four letters that, depending on ...
content:
There is a particular kind of Zambian political mishap that does not require a scandal, a leaked memo, or a court case. It only requires a microphone, a moment of irritation, and one word too many. This week’s lesson in the genre comes courtesy of the word “boys”, four letters that, depending on who says them and when, can mean affection, dismissal, or a declaration of war you did not know you’d started.

Permit a small detour, because it is instructive. Growing up, there is a particular confidence that comes from having won most of your fights. You develop a sense, entirely unearned, that size and seniority are the same thing. Then one day a boy younger and smaller than you says something spectacularly unprintable, the kind of insult that seems engineered specifically to end friendships, and you decide, reasonably you think, that this will be a quick lesson in respect.

It was not quick. It was not a lesson, certainly not the one intended. By the end of it, the only person who needed a shower was the one who started the fight, and it was not the small boy. The story is funny now, decades later, precisely because of how wrong the prediction was. Underestimating someone because they are younger, smaller, or simply more recently arrived in the room is one of the oldest mistakes there is, and politics has never once been an exception to it.

This is worth remembering whenever a politician reaches for “boys” as a put-down. History, and certain childhood afternoons, suggest the boys tend to remember exactly who called them that.

Here is a stubborn arithmetic fact that no amount of branding can solve: a crowd that gathers because it wants to is not the same animal as a crowd that gathers because it was organised, transported, fed, and handed a T-shirt. Both can look identical in a photograph. Only one of them tends to show up again, unprompted, for free, simply because it wants to be there.

Organic crowds are inconvenient for exactly this reason. They cannot be procured, only earned, and earning is slower and less controllable than procurement. But the deeper trap is the assumption that paying for a crowd buys the vote that comes with it. It does not. A rented audience is, by definition, available to the highest subsequent bidder. Anyone who has organised a rally knows the quiet dread of wondering how many people in the front row are simply waiting for the bus fare to be confirmed before deciding their actual opinion on anything.

The trendy assumption is that influence is a one directional good: more reach, more visibility, more momentum. But the honest question was never “do they have influence”, it was always “in which direction does that influence run.” A figure who is broadly disliked does not merely fail to help when paraded on a stage; they actively manufacture opposition in rooms that were previously undecided. People who had no opinion on a candidate will happily form one the moment they see who that candidate is standing next to.

This is the same logic behind a phenomenon familiar to every Zambian election cycle: the parade of endorsements from political figures whose own standing has seen better decades. There is a particular desperation signalled when a campaign reaches for a once prominent, now largely discredited former leader and presents the handshake as a coup. It rarely reads as strength. It reads, more often, as a campaign counting its remaining options and discovering the cupboard increasingly bare, the last kicks, as the saying goes, of a horse that has already sensed how this race ends.

And then there is timing, which is its own separate discipline entirely from substance.

The Kafwaya remark concerning Barotseland will likely take a while to fully cool down, not because the underlying idea was indefensible, but because it was delivered at precisely the moment guaranteed to make it land as dismissal rather than discussion. There is a real, serious conversation to be had about an international airport for Western Province, and a reasonable case that such infrastructure could meaningfully boost the regional economy once broader economic groundwork is laid. None of that is the problem. The problem is sequencing: saying a true and useful thing at the exact wrong hour, in the exact wrong tone, to an audience already primed to hear insult rather than investment.

Outside election season, that statement might have been received as policy. Inside it, delivered the way it was, it was received as a closed door, and closed doors, in Barotseland politics, do not reopen quickly or quietly. A sincere apology, offered without conditions attached, would do more right now than any infrastructure pledge could.

There is a pleasing, almost poetic justice in how Kafwaya’s own name became the punchline here.Ukufwayafwaya, to go looking, searching, poking about with more enthusiasm than sense, is exactly what the name suggests he went and did. A sleeping lion does not need to be woken. The wise traveller walks around it, admires it from a distance, perhaps takes a photograph. Only the monkey, supremely confident in its own cleverness, decides the lion’s most sensitive parts are worth a closer poke. The lion, it should be said, eventually wakes up. It always does. And it does not wake up amused.

If there is a moral here, and there is always a moral, even in stories that begin with a childhood scuffle and end with a lion being poked, it arrives in two parts.

The first:do not look down on anyone. Size, youth, and apparent harmlessness are terrible predictors of outcome. Football reminded everyone of this just yesterday, when Germany and the Netherlands, two footballing institutions, two names that alone used to be enough to settle a tie before kick off, were both knocked out by opponents the script had not accounted for. Reputation is not a result. It never was.

The second:be careful whose support you accept, because endorsement is not always the gift it appears to be. Without naming names, the reader will know exactly who is meant, there are, in this particular story, two figures whose backing tends to do more harm than good. Two are simply unpopular outright, disliked by enough Zambians that their presence on a stage costs more votes than it buys. The third is a sadder case: a man who once brought genuinely good news to this country, who should by rights be remembered as a source of wisdom and hope, but who, through the accumulated weight of public resentment, has been worn down into something closer to a parrot, repeating lines rather than offering counsel. Hate did that. It has a way of turning trees into puppets.

Underestimate no one. Accept endorsement from no one merely because they are loud. And whatever you do, leave the sleeping lion alone.

title: Farmers, agribusinesses report growth under EU-backed financing through local banks
summary: ZAMBIAN farmers and agricultural small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) are reporting increased production, improved resilience to climate shocks and expanded market opportunities after accessing financing through the Zambia Agriculture Value Chain Facility (ZAVCF).
content:
ZAMBIAN farmers and agricultural small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) are reporting increased production, improved resilience to climate shocks and expanded market opportunities after accessing financing through the Zambia Agriculture Value Chain Facility (ZAVCF).

The facility, supported by the European Investment Bank (EIB Global) and the European Union, was introduced to address one of the biggest challenges in Zambia’s agriculture sector, which is limited access to affordable and long-term financing.

For many farmers and agribusinesses, lack of financial support has hindered their expansion, investment in irrigation systems and adoption of modern technologies, despite agriculture employing between 50 and 70 percent of the country’s population.

At Matuka Farm in Mkushi, the farm manager Joyce Nambeye said funding through the ZAVCF allowed the business to expand operations, set up an irrigation system and remain productive in the face of challenges like the worst drought in living memory during the 2023/2024 farming season.

The drought also led to the disruption in powered supply since Zambia is heavily dependent on hydropower, which meant a lot of loadshedding.

Nambeye said weather changes and power shortages threatened the farm’s survival until it accessed financing through Zanaco, one of the local banks partnering in the facility.

“We saw an advert on social media and approached Zanaco which explained the programme to us. After getting the loan, we were able to invest in an irrigation system and we are now making un-interrupted progress,” she said.

In Kabwe, Amadeus International managing director Rajendra Patel said access to financing was previously a challenge but through ZAVCF and Zanaco, the company was able to install additional irrigation infrastructure and expand cultivated land.

“The funding helped us introduce more irrigation, expand hectares and even do double cropping on the same land. It also protects us from droughts and improves our revenues,” Patel said.

Through the ZAVCF partnership with local Zambian banks, commercial farms have also benefited from the facility’s provision of foreign-currency loans, which are suited to export-based operations.

Galaunia Farm managing director Nigel Lacey said getting loans in US dollars aligns better with farms that sell their products in international markets or need to import inputs or equipment as they can pay in foreign currency.

“Borrowing in dollars at a viable interest rate allows us to remain profitable and continue supporting our workers and the surrounding community,” he said.

The Zambia Agriculture Value Chain Facility was launched in 2021 as part of the EU’s Global Gateway target to support sustainable development and economic transformation through targeted investments.

Through the lending framework, the European Investment Bank channels capital to local banks, including Zanaco, First Capital Bank Zambia and the Zambia Industrial Commercial Bank, who then lend to farmers and agribusinesses that understand local market conditions.

Since the programme’s launch, multiple credit lines have been mobilised, including €15 million extended to Zanaco in 2021, €10 million to First Capital Bank in 2023 and a €15 million facility signed with Zambia Industrial Commercial Bank in 2025 to support private sector agricultural investment.

The Zanaco credit line is already fully utilised with strong tangible impact being witnessed on the ground. This also shows the big need for affordable financing in the market.

The ZAVCF combines long-term financing, risk-sharing guarantees backed by the European Union and technical assistance to strengthen both banks and borrowers.

By sharing credit risk with local banks, the programme acts as a safety net, enabling financial institutions to extend loans to farmers who previously lacked sufficient collateral or smaller sized companies that were previously seen as too risky to support.

Beyond financing, head of the European Investment Bank’s Hub for Southern Africa, Jim Hodges said the facility provides training and technical assistance to farmers to improve productivity, environmental sustainability and business management.

“We provide financing and technical assistance to private sector development projects in Zambia, which support the national key priorities as well as aligning to the EU objectives,” said Hodges.

Meanwhile, the European Union ambassador to Zambia and COMESA Karolina Stasiak said the EU will continue supporting Zambia’s ambition to transform the agriculture sector into a thriving sector that creates jobs and boosts growth for the country.

“Our actions focus primarily on supporting small-scale farmers and fishermen to modernise their farming practices and to connect them with businesses.”

“This includes promoting new investments in promising agricultural value chains. This includes also facilitating access to finance, to capacity building, and to inputs,” stated Stasiak.

title: Government to host expo for make up artists, hair dressers, beauty professionals
summary: GOVERNMENT says it will this year host a national cosmetology expo aimed at promoting skills, creativity and business growth in the beauty industry.
content:
GOVERNMENT says it will this year host a national cosmetology expo aimed at promoting skills, creativity and business growth in the beauty industry.

Permanent secretary in the Ministry of Small and Medium Enterprise Development Subeta Mutelo said the event will provide a platform for make up artists, hairdressers and nail technicians to showcase their products and services.

Speaking at an Entrepreneurs Business Brunch in Lusaka yesterday, hosted by businesswoman Sarah Mako, Mutelo said the expo is intended to elevate the cosmetology sector and create new opportunities for practitioners.

“We want to create a platform where make-up artists, hairdressers and nail technicians can come forward and showcase their skills, their products and their creativity,” she said.

She added that the initiative is part of broader efforts to recognise skills-based professions as viable sources of income and employment.

“We all need to have a skill, and cosmetology is one of the fastest-growing sectors. This expo will not only boost businesses but also encourage innovation and confidence among young people,” Mutelo said.

In a light remark, she added: “I actually did my own make up and it shows that these are skills anyone can learn and turn into a business.”

Meanwhile, Mutelo urged entrepreneurs to take advantage of the K515 million set aside this year to support micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs).

She encouraged entrepreneurs to engage directly with the ministry to better understand how to access funding and other forms of support.

She observed that most entrepreneurs do not read or research, hence, opportunities seem to be far fetched for them.

“This year alone, government has allocated K515 million to support MSMEs. This shows how central small businesses are to our economic growth agenda,” she said.

“The ministry is here for you. We urge business owners to visit our offices, ask questions and position themselves to benefit from these opportunities,” she said.

Mutelo noted that many entrepreneurs miss out on support due to a lack of information or reluctance to engage with institutions.

“Most entrepreneurs are not aware of the support systems available, while others hesitate to come forward. These resources are meant for you, and we want you to benefit,” she said.

She also stressed the importance of formalising businesses and maintaining proper records.

“You need to prepare your business for growth. Register it, keep proper books and be ready when opportunities arise,” she added.

The event brought together entrepreneurs from across Zambia, offering a platform for networking, mentorship and the exchange of ideas.

title: Blood on the campaign trail: two dead as ECZ threatens suspensions
summary: The Electoral Commission of Zambia (ECZ) has warned that it will suspend any candidate or political party found responsible for the deaths of two people in Kaputa and Mazabuka districts, as police continue investigating a wave of violence that has struck the campaign trail six weeks before the Au...
content:
The Electoral Commission of Zambia (ECZ) has warned that it will suspend any candidate or political party found responsible for the deaths of two people in Kaputa and Mazabuka districts, as police continue investigating a wave of violence that has struck the campaign trail six weeks before the August 13 general election.

ECZ Chief Electoral Officer Brown Kasaro said the Commission was awaiting the conclusion of police investigations before exercising its powers under the Electoral Process (Amendment) Act No. 12 of 2026. He said the Commission strongly condemned the electoral violence that had claimed two lives and called for those responsible to be brought to justice.

In Mazabuka, Stanley Sakala, 31, a driver for independent Magoye East parliamentary candidate Confucius Mweene, was found dead inside his chalet at a campaign camp in Chivuna Village.

Southern Province Police Commanding Officer Moono Namalongo said Sakala and the candidate returned from campaign activities at about 20:00 hours on June 29 before parking their vehicle at the camp.

Police said Sakala was later seen in the company of another man before the two returned to the camp at around 22:30 hours. The man later went to his own residence while Sakala retired alone to his chalet.

At about midnight, a female juvenile staying at the camp reportedly heard sounds of a struggle coming from the chalet and alerted her father, who, together with other people, went to investigate. They found Sakala lying in a pool of blood inside the room.

Police have since detained a 26-year-old man from Mwanakuweza Village in Chief Hanjalika’s area. Namalongo said the suspect was the last known person seen with Sakala before his death, leading investigators to place him in custody as inquiries continue.

In Kaputa District, police have arrested ten people in connection with the death of a 25-year-old UPND supporter, Kwesha Ng’onga, who was allegedly killed during a clash involving suspected opposition party cadres.

Police spokesperson Godfrey Chilabi said a post-mortem examination had been conducted before the victim was buried. He added that the security situation in Kaputa had stabilised and was now under control.

Chilabi said reinforcements from the Zambia Police School of Public Order Maintenance in Kamfinsa, Kitwe, had been deployed to support ongoing operations, while efforts to identify and apprehend additional suspects in both investigations were continuing.

Kasaro said the Commission now had legal authority to suspend political parties or candidates found to have breached the Electoral Code of Conduct through acts of political violence. He urged the Zambia Police Service to expedite investigations into both killings and ensure that all perpetrators faced the law.

The Mazabuka and Kaputa killings have been accompanied by other reports of campaign-related violence across the country.

Socialist Party president Fred M’membe alleged that Lufwanyama East parliamentary candidate Moses Chiyuka was attacked on Tuesday by UPND cadres travelling in a campaign convoy, sustaining an eye injury while the windscreen of his vehicle was smashed.

He said the incident followed an earlier attack in Kanyama Constituency in which People’s Pact candidate Harriet Katanekwa was allegedly assaulted and her phone stolen, as well as a weekend incident in which cadres damaged the vehicle of Socialist Party Kanyama candidate Brighton Bilumba during a party rally.

M’membe blamed the continued violence on what he described as a culture of impunity, arguing that the attacks persisted because those responsible believed they would not be held accountable.

The Paralegal Alliance Network also condemned the recent incidents, with chairperson Eugene Kabalika calling on political leaders and law enforcement agencies to take decisive action.

Kabalika said the organisation was deeply saddened by the loss of life in Mazabuka and Kaputa, warning that political violence undermines Zambia’s democratic institutions, restricts civic space and instils fear among citizens. He urged political parties to resolve differences through peaceful dialogue rather than confrontation and called on the Zambia Police Service to investigate all perpetrators impartially, regardless of political affiliation.

Anti-Political Violence Association of Zambia president Bernadette Mulenga similarly condemned the bloodshed, describing it as shameful for a Christian nation. She appealed to government, churches and cooperating partners to support community sensitisation programmes aimed at preventing further political violence.

Meanwhile, President Hakainde Hichilema, through State House Chief Communications Specialist Clayson Hamasaka, directed security agencies to investigate and prosecute everyone responsible for the violence in Kaputa and Mazabuka.

The President said the law applies equally to all citizens regardless of political affiliation and stressed that anyone found perpetrating violence would face the courts as an individual, without protection from any political party.

title: Increase the number of vaccinated eligible adults from the current less than 3% to at least 70%, in order to achieve “herd immunity” says Dr Jonas Chanda.
summary: PRESS STATEMENT FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE
content:
PRESS STATEMENT FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE

POLITICISING COVID-19 FIGHT WILL BE DISASTROUS FOR ZAMBIA

Ndola, Zambia. Monday, 4th October 2021

Since the first Covid-19 cases were reported in Zambia in March 2020, the country has recorded209,163 caseswith3,649 citizens dyingfrom this deadly disease. These are not mere statistics but our fathers, mothers, brothers, sisters, and children.

Sadly only less than3%of the eligible adult population is vaccinated against Covid-19 even when a“Fourth (4th) Wave”of the pandemic is imminent. The“4th Wave”may overwhelm or even collapse the country’s healthcare system with hospital admissions and deaths, anf may lead to deadly socioeconomic consequences.

Globally,232,245,651Covid-19 cases have been recorded with total number ofdeaths approaching 5 million.The most developed country in the world, the USA, is the world’s most affected country accounting for15%of the world’s cases, andover 700,000 deaths.And despite the availability of Covid-19 vaccines there,70 millionAmericans remain unvaccinated. We have also seen the devastating health and socioeconomic impact of Covid-19 in European countries, and in India, Brazil, and African countries.

Since 16th August 2021 when the last election results were announced, there has beenhuge lapsein adherence to Covid-19 public health prevention measures, and everything has been literally “OPEN” since then. Life immediately went back to normal as bars, restaurants, markets, churches, shops, public meetings, sports and social events all operated as before Covid-19. In addition, very few of our citizens are wearing face masks in public even today. So the recent pronouncements by government about “OPENING-UP” of these public places is self-contradictory because these public places have been “WIDE OPEN” since 16th August.

I appeal to politicians to stop politicking with Covid-19 and instead allow the technocrats at the Ministry of Health and Zambia National Public Health Institute to take the lead. Mixed messages from politicians have contributed to Covid-19 vaccine hesitancy by Zambians. One day politicians will call Covid-19 a “hoax”, and tell people not to get Covid-19 vaccination. The next day, they will turn around and say Covid-19 is “real” and ask people to get vaccinated. These contradictions lead to loss of trust by the people. And politicians claiming to be vaccinated must lead by example and do so in public, not in private as people will not believe them.

I urge government to refocus their energies on policy measures that will mitigate the deadly impact of the upcoming4th Wave of the Covid-19 pandemic, including strict enforcement of and adherence to Covid-19 public health prevention measures such as wearing of face masks in public. They should also outline strategies they are putting in place to increase the number of vaccinated eligible adults from the current less than 3% to at least 70%, in order to achieve“herd immunity.”The only way out of the Covid-19 pandemic is strict adherence to the Covid-19 prevention measures and Covid-19 vaccination for the majority of our people.

I also appeal to our Traditional and Church leaders to take the lead in sensitizing people against Covid-19 and encouraging them to get Covid-19 vaccinations since they are more likely to be believed by the public compared to politicians.

Dr Jonas Chanda,Former Minister of Health

title: ZAMBIA kisses goodbye to FIFA CAF World Cup
summary: Has the Chipolopolo loses 1-0 to Niger, ending with only nine points, marking one of their worst World Cup campaigns in years
content:
Has the Chipolopolo loses 1-0 to Niger, ending with only nine points, marking one of their worst World Cup campaigns in years

THE Chipolopolo signed off the 2026 FIFA CAF World Cup qualifier on anticlimax after losing to Niger at Levy Mwanawasa Stadium yesterday.The Chipolopolo who had raised hope of a decent finish in Group E following their 1-0 triumph over Tanzania in Zanzibar last Wednesday, were on fire in the first half in which they dominated the visitors.Coach Avram Grant set the tone for an all-out attack by fielding Edward Chilufya, Fashion Sakala, Patson Daka and Lameck Banda in the starting up.The Chipolopolo went all out for goals from the first whistle but were wasteful in front of goal.Zambia could have gone ahead as early as fourth minute but Kings Kangwa couldn’t yield anything from corner.  Three minutes later, Zambia’s real chance falls to Fashion Sakala whose thumping effort went inches over the bar.  In the 10 minute, it was Patson Daka turn as he tried his luck from outside the box but his shot goes wide.The Chipolopolo continued patronising the visitor’s goal with Kelvin Kapumbu coming close with a header from a Gift Mphande throw in.  Daka’s header in the 21st minute went wide from a well worked move from Sakala and Lubambo Musonda.  Five minutes later,  Lameck Banda came close but his shot went off target.Kangwa couldn’t believe seeing his effort roll wide after being teed by Patson Daka in the 34th minute.Kangwa was guilty yet again when he wasted a free kick for Zambia after Lameck Banda was brought down eight months to half time.It was Niger’s time to test the Zambian goal mouth Oumarou Aliou striking from long range with Lawrence Mulenga parrying it away for a corner.After surviving the first half onslaught, Niger found the back of the Chipolopolo net 10 minutes in the second half through 𝐀𝐧𝐭𝐨𝐧𝐲 𝐃𝐚𝐧𝐢𝐞𝐥 𝐒𝐨𝐬𝐚𝐡.The Chipolopolo tried all the tricks in the book but failed to breach the Nigerien goal.After scoring, the Nigeriens raised their tempo and took the game to the Chipolopolo half.Niger’s victory pushed them to 15 points while Zambia remain at nine points – marking one of their worst World Cup campaigns in years.

title: ECZ dismisses remarked ballot allegations
summary: THE Electoral Commission of Zambia (ECZ) has dismissed allegations that it plans to print extra ballot papers and has instead challenged those making the accusation to provide proof.
content:
THE Electoral Commission of Zambia (ECZ) has dismissed allegations that it plans to print extra ballot papers and has instead challenged those making the accusation to provide proof.

The allegations, contained in a widely circulated advisory note, claim that ECZ officials, representatives of Dubai-based Al Ghurair Printing and Publishing, and officials from the Zanzibar Government Printing Agency held meetings to print extra pre-marked ballot papers for selected constituencies in Eastern, Southern and Central provinces.

This is meant o manipulate the August 13 general election in favour of President Hakainde Hichilema.

The advisory also alleges that millions of United States dollars were paid to facilitate the purported operation and names several individuals allegedly involved in the scheme.

However, ECZ Corporate Affairs Manager Patricia Luhanga dismissed the claims, saying those making the allegations must provide evidence.

“The burden of proof is on those who have issued you with this advisory,” Ms Luhanga said.

She added that the commission has consistently informed stakeholders about the ballot printing process and that it is being conducted transparently.

“The commission is on record to having informed all stakeholders that ballot papers are being printed in Dubai and being witnessed by various stakeholders. This is public information,” Ms Luhanga said.

ECZ has repeatedly maintained that representatives of political parties, civil society organisations and other accredited stakeholders are observing the ballot printing exercise to ensure transparency and accountability.

It has urged the public to rely on verified information from official sources and cautioned against the spread of unsubstantiated claims that could undermine public confidence in the electoral process.

Investors have regained appetite for Zambia after default, Citi says

LIVEMikalo Finance Official Relaunch

LIVEMikalo Finance Official Relaunch

LIVEZAFFICO Bell Ringing Ceremony | Rights Issue

LIVESun King | Powering Access to Brighter Lives – Ndola

LIVESun King | Powering Access to Brighter Lives – Ndola

LIVEACC | World Whistleblower Day 2026

Your email address will not be published.Required fields are marked*

Save my name, email, and website in this browser for the next time I comment.

Zambia’s mining, energy and agriculture sectors have drawn significant investor interest as the government’s fiscal consolidation gains traction after emerging from a sovereign default, said Citi’s country chief.

“We have noted increased interest, especially when we have seen significant strides being made in terms of bringing stability to the Zambian economy,” said Lowani Chibesakunda, Citi’s chief executive and banking head for Zambia, in an interview with Reuters on Tuesday.

“We have noted key interests in mining, in energy, as well as in agriculture.”

Zambia is rich in copper, positioning it well as demand for the red metal grows, spurred by the transition to clean energy and soaring power needs for AI data centres.

“You cannot talk about the Zambian story without emphasising the importance of mining and seeing how copper prices have remained bullish,” said Chibesakunda.

“The focus that is there now on copper-related products, be it your electric vehicles, et cetera, is really driving a lot of growth in that sector.”

The government secured 97.85% participation in a cash tender offer for its $1.365 billion outstanding notes, launched on May 29, in what the Ministry of Finance described as part of broader efforts to improve debt sustainability and reduce long-term obligations. Citi acted as sole mandated bank to organize, structure, and execute the transaction.

In November, S&P Global upgraded Zambia’s long- and short-term foreign-currency credit rating to CCC+/C from SD (selective default) with a stable outlook, formally removing the country’s default status.

The upgrade followed the government’s forecast of a rosier fiscal picture for 2026, estimating the budget deficit would more than halve and targeting economic growth of more than 6% after years of protracted debt restructuring and a severe drought.

Chibesakunda said she was also seeing new categories of investors entering the market for the first time.

“We have seen increased interest from players coming through from the Middle East … just to give you a sense, the pharmaceutical sector, the technology space.” In mining, “we’ve had players coming into this market that are mining for the first time,” she said.

The broader investment surge is reflected in the data. According to United Nations trade data, Zambia attracted $1.24 billion in foreign direct investment in 2024, the highest inflow since 2015.

title: ZRA seizes smuggled heavy-duty grader in Kapiri Mposhi, prevents K615,000 revenue loss
summary: THE Zambia Revenue Authority (ZRA) has seized a smuggled heavy-duty self-propelled motor grader and the commercial truck used to transport it in Kapiri Mposhi, preventing a revenue loss of more than K615,000.
content:
THE Zambia Revenue Authority (ZRA) has seized a smuggled heavy-duty self-propelled motor grader and the commercial truck used to transport it in Kapiri Mposhi, preventing a revenue loss of more than K615,000.

The interception occurred on Tuesday, 23 June 2026, during anti-smuggling patrols along the Northern corridor, ZRA said Wednesday.

ZRA Corporate Communications Manager Oliver Nzala said the driver presented customs documents that appeared legitimate but belonged to a different consignment. To mislead officers, smugglers used a truck whose registration number matched the fake documents, while the trailer carrying the grader had a different registration number as a disguise to bypass quick checkpoints.

“The Zambia Revenue Authority has officially seized the motor grader, the truck and the trailer used to carry it,” Nzala said. The seizure was made under the Customs and Excise Act, Chapter 322 of the Laws of Zambia.

He said ZRA remains on high alert to curb smuggling and urged taxpayers to avoid the practice to prevent forfeiture and prosecution.

(Mwebantu, Friday, 26th June, 2026)

title: Police Arrest Man Over Assault on Chinese Road Supervisor
summary: POLICE on the Copperbelt have arrested a 32-year-old Kitwe driver after a Chinese supervisor was assaulted at an AVIC construction site, the Police Service said July 2.
content:
POLICE on the Copperbelt have arrested a 32-year-old Kitwe driver after a Chinese supervisor was assaulted at an AVIC construction site, the Police Service said July 2.

The incident occurred June 28 around 10:30 a.m. at the AVIC International Camp Site along the Mufulira–Mokambo Road, Public Relations Officer Godfrey Chilabi said.

Shao Hong, 46, supervisor for the road project, reported he was checking on tipper truck drivers who reported late in Murundu. After suspecting alcohol use, he administered breathalyser tests. Police said some drivers became unruly and Wana Chambalu allegedly punched and kicked Shao, causing injuries.

Chambalu is in custody charged with Assault Occasioning Actual Bodily Harm, OABH, Chilabi said. Police “strongly condemn all forms of violence, including workplace-related violence,” and said offenders will face court “regardless of nationality, occupation, or status.”

(Mwebantu, Friday, 3rd July, 2026)

A moderate cold wave warning is in effect for most of Zambia from Friday, 26th June to Saturday, 27th June 2026, weather officials said.

A strong high-pressure system is expected to bring increasingly windy and cool conditions nationwide, with lower temperatures, dusty conditions, reduced visibility, and hazardous gusty winds.

Cloudy conditions are forecast over Eastern and Lusaka provinces, which are the primary areas of concern.

Authorities advised the general public to keep warm and take extra precautions, especially for vulnerable groups and people with respiratory conditions.

Water transport operators were urged to suspend operations or exercise extreme caution, while farmers were told to protect sensitive crops and secure loose structures and equipment.

(Mwebantu, Thursday, 25th June, 2026)

A 34-year-old man was treated at Levy Mwanawasa University Teaching Hospital this week for a penile fracture, a rare urological emergency requiring urgent surgical intervention, hospital officials said.

According to LMUTH, the injury occurred during sexual activity earlier in the week. Clinical assessment showed a significant penile hematoma with injury to the corpus spongiosum and complete transection of the penile urethra. The patient also reported prior use of energy-boosting drinks.

The patient was taken to theatre where urologists performed urgent surgical repair, including circumcision. The hospital said the procedure was successful and the patient is stable and recovering well.

The surgical team included urologists Dr. Muleya M, Dr. Mufaya, Dr. Lupinda C.K, and Dr. Chungu. Anaesthesia was administered by Ms. Lydia with Sr. Vivian as scrub nurse.

LMUTH said the case highlights risks associated with unregulated substances marketed for sexual enhancement, which may contribute to unsafe practices and serious health complications. The hospital urged the public to seek guidance from qualified healthcare professionals regarding sexual health concerns.

“LMUTH remains committed to delivering timely, specialized, and life-saving surgical care,” the hospital said.

(Mwebantu, Thursday, 25th June, 2026)

FILM producer and director David Kazadi, model Mwaka Halwindi, and Ruby Muzandu have pleaded not guilty to charges of producing, possessing and circulating obscene material.

The trio appeared before Lusaka Magistrates’ Court today for plea taking before Lusaka Magistrate Kawama Mwamfuli.

Mr Kazadi and Ms Halwindi denied the charges they jointly face.

Ms Muzandu also pleaded not guilty to circulating and possessing the said material.

The State alleges that between January 2023 and June 2025, Ms Muzandu circulated the alleged obscene material through a WhatsApp group known as “Trip to South Africa”.

Mr Kazadi is accused of sharing videos involving Ms Halwindi and other alleged explicit content within the same period.

After the plea, the court adjourned the matter to 24 and 25 August 2026 for commencement of trial.

(Mwebantu, Thursday, 25th June, 2026)

POLICE in Lusaka have detained two female juveniles aged 15 and 16 in connection with the suspected murder of 18-year-old Taonga Phiri of Kamwala South, Lusaka.

Zambia Police Public Relations Officer, Godfrey Chilabi, disclosed that the matter was reported to police on 22nd June 2026 around 20:30 hours by Michael Malambo, 40, who reported the death of his daughter under suspicious circumstances.

He added that preliminary investigations indicate that on 21 June 2026 between 08:00 and 11:00 hours, the 15-year-old juvenile now in custody contacted her mother and informed her that the deceased had collapsed at a residence in Kamwala South.

She also alerted a nearby relative who found the victim lying unconscious and unresponsive. The victim was rushed to the University Teaching Hospital where medical personnel pronounced her dead on arrival.

Investigations so far suggest the deceased may have been assaulted and strangled. It is further alleged that unknown tablets were administered after the incident in an attempt to make the death appear to be suicide.

Following further inquiries, police detained both the 15-year-old and 16-year-old juveniles in connection with the matter.

The body of the deceased has been deposited in the UTH Mortuary pending a post-mortem examination to determine the exact cause of death. The motive has not yet been established and investigations remain ongoing.

Police say they will provide further updates as investigations progress.

(Mwebantu, Wednesday, 24th June, 2026)

title: Lusaka man breaks his ‘package’ during sex, doctors warn on enhancement products.
summary: A 34-year-old man was treated at Levy Mwanawasa University Teaching Hospital this week for a penile fracture, a rare urological emergency requiring urgent surgical intervention, hospital officials said.
content:
A 34-year-old man was treated at Levy Mwanawasa University Teaching Hospital this week for a penile fracture, a rare urological emergency requiring urgent surgical intervention, hospital officials said.

According to LMUTH, the injury occurred during sexual activity earlier in the week. Clinical assessment showed a significant penile hematoma with injury to the corpus spongiosum and complete transection of the penile urethra. The patient also reported prior use of energy-boosting drinks.

The patient was taken to theatre where urologists performed urgent surgical repair, including circumcision. The hospital said the procedure was successful and the patient is stable and recovering well.

The surgical team included urologists Dr. Muleya M, Dr. Mufaya, Dr. Lupinda C.K, and Dr. Chungu. Anaesthesia was administered by Ms. Lydia with Sr. Vivian as scrub nurse.

LMUTH said the case highlights risks associated with unregulated substances marketed for sexual enhancement, which may contribute to unsafe practices and serious health complications. The hospital urged the public to seek guidance from qualified healthcare professionals regarding sexual health concerns.

“LMUTH remains committed to delivering timely, specialized, and life-saving surgical care,” the hospital said.

(Mwebantu, Thursday, 25th June, 2026)

Cold wave warning issued for most of Zambia, including Lusaka

THE Government of the Republic of Zambia has called for renewed global solidarity and sustainable financing to sustain progress in the fight against HIV and AIDS and end the epidemic as a public health threat by 2030.

This is according to a press statement issued by Namatama Njekwa, First Secretary for Press and Public Relations at Permanent Mission of the Republic of Zambia to the United Nations.

Delivering Zambia’s national statement at the 2026 High-Level Meeting on HIV/AIDS on Monday, 23 June 2026, Ministry of Health Permanent Secretary Dr. George Sinyangwe reaffirmed Zambia’s commitment to work with the international community to reduce HIV to levels where it ceases to be a public health threat.

Dr. Sinyangwe highlighted that Zambia has attained UNAIDS’ epidemiological targets of 98-98-97. This reflects 98% of people living with HIV know their status, 98% of those diagnosed are on treatment, and 97% of those on treatment have viral suppression.

Despite the gains, he noted Zambia still faces challenges: rising HIV incidence among young people, emergence of drug resistance, growing burden of non-communicable diseases, and contraction in external financing.

“Failure to renew global commitment to the HIV response could lead to increased drug resistance, premature deaths, reduced life expectancy and loss of productivity,” Dr. Sinyangwe warned. He appealed for global leaders to unite as they did two decades ago to pool funding for HIV.

He also showcased Zambia’s growing capacity in training doctors and nurses, and expressed readiness to collaborate with other nations on health workforce development.

Opening the meeting, UN General Assembly President H.E. Annalena Baerbock said multilateral action has turned HIV from a death sentence into a manageable condition, but progress is threatened by shrinking funding. UN Deputy Secretary-General Amina Mohammed stressed the need to close treatment gaps, protect human rights, and secure sustainable financing. UNAIDS Executive Director Winnie Byanyima warned that declining development assistance and shrinking civic space could undermine efforts to end AIDS.

Zambia’s delegation is led by Dr. Sinyangwe and includes Permanent Representative Dr. Chola Milambo and National AIDS Council Director General Dr. Kebby Musokotwane. The High-Level Meeting is convened every five years since 2001 and reviews progress toward the 2021 Political Declaration on HIV/AIDS while negotiating a new declaration to guide the next five years.

(Mwebantu, Wednesday, 24th June, 2026)

THE Zambian government has described remarks attributed to outgoing United States Ambassador Michael Gonzales as undiplomatic and inconsistent with mutual respect between sovereign nations.

This follows a farewell message delivered by Gonzales during a reception held in his honour on April 30, 2026, at the Ambassador’s official residence in Lusaka.

Last week, Gonzales warned of what he termed “institutionalised and sophisticated corruption” in Zambia, suggesting that the government’s anti-corruption stance was rhetorical rather than effective.

Speaking during a media briefing, Foreign Affairs Minister Mulambo Haimbe said the assertions were “deeply regrettable, undiplomatic, and inconsistent with the principles governing diplomatic relations between sovereign states.”

He added that the statement contravenes the spirit of the Vienna Convention on Diplomatic Relations, which outlines the expected conduct of diplomats in receiving states.

“Further, and characteristic of the outgoing Ambassador’s approach during his tour of duty, the remarks, which were delivered during a social event, were not communicated to the Ministry of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation through an official complaint, demarche, or any formal channel. This underscores a lack of respect for the receiving state and established diplomatic processes,” Haimbe said.

Haimbe also emphasised that Zambia’s relationship with the United States is not, and has never been, centred on aid.

He clarified that stalled negotiations on the Health Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) are due to provisions the Zambian government considers unacceptable, particularly those relating to data sharing that may violate citizens’ right to privacy.

He added that some of the matters are currently subject to litigation in Zambian courts and must be respected, noting that certain provisions are viewed as unconscionable from the perspective of the Zambian people.

Haimbe said the government remains committed to engaging in good-faith negotiations with all strategic partners, including the United States, particularly in areas such as critical minerals beneficiation, in a value-adding environment for the mutual benefit of Zambia and its partners.

(Mwebantu, Monday, 4th May, 2026)

ZAMBIA has recorded progress in sexual and reproductive health, with demand for family planning satisfied by modern methods rising from 19.6 percent in 1992 to 73.9 percent in 2024.

Skilled birth attendance has also increased to 94 percent, while facility-based deliveries now stand at 93 percent, reflecting sustained Government efforts to improve access to quality health services.

Speaking during the 59th Session of the Commission on Population and Development (CPD) at the United Nations Headquarters, Chargé d’Affaires at Zambia’s Mission to the UN, Ms Stenah Shampile, said the progress underscores the country’s commitment to universal health coverage and equitable service delivery.

She said Zambia remains committed to the International Conference on Population and Development (ICPD) Programme of Action, which prioritises human rights, social justice, and the empowerment of women and young people.

Ms Shampile emphasised that technology, research and data are key drivers of sustainable development, noting that Zambia’s engagement in global platforms helps strengthen partnerships and promote the exchange of evidence-based solutions.

According to a statement issued by First Secretary for Press and Public Relations at the Permanent Mission of the Republic of Zambia, Ms Namatama Njekwa, Zambia’s participation in the session reflects its continued commitment to multilateral engagement and advancing the global development agenda.

Ms Shampile highlighted the country’s adoption of geo-referenced and gender-responsive Carrying Capacity Assessments (CCA), which map social services across all 116 districts and integrate population data with gender analysis to enhance planning and coordination.

However, she acknowledged that challenges persist, including an adolescent childbearing rate of 28 percent and maternal mortality, which accounts for 10 percent of deaths among women of reproductive age.

She said Government is addressing these issues through expanded voluntary family planning services and strengthened adolescent-focused programmes aimed at achieving universal access to sexual and reproductive health.

On digital transformation, Ms Shampile said Zambia is integrating information and communication technology (ICT) in education under the Ministry of Education’s 2022–2026 Strategic Plan, with digital platforms increasingly reaching rural communities and improving access to reproductive health information.

Ms Shampile was accompanied by Ministry of Finance and National Planning Director for Population, Ms Pamela Kauseni.

The meeting, which opened on April 13, ends on April 17, 2026, under the theme “Population, technology and research in the context of sustainable development.”

(Mwebantu, Friday, 17th April, 2026)

title: David Kazadi, Mwaka Halwindi, and Co-Accused Plead Not Guilty to Obscene Material Charges. Case adjourned to 24-25 August 2026.
summary: FILM producer and director David Kazadi, model Mwaka Halwindi, and Ruby Muzandu have pleaded not guilty to charges of producing, possessing and circulating obscene material.
content:
FILM producer and director David Kazadi, model Mwaka Halwindi, and Ruby Muzandu have pleaded not guilty to charges of producing, possessing and circulating obscene material.

The trio appeared before Lusaka Magistrates’ Court today for plea taking before Lusaka Magistrate Kawama Mwamfuli.

Mr Kazadi and Ms Halwindi denied the charges they jointly face.

Ms Muzandu also pleaded not guilty to circulating and possessing the said material.

The State alleges that between January 2023 and June 2025, Ms Muzandu circulated the alleged obscene material through a WhatsApp group known as “Trip to South Africa”.

Mr Kazadi is accused of sharing videos involving Ms Halwindi and other alleged explicit content within the same period.

After the plea, the court adjourned the matter to 24 and 25 August 2026 for commencement of trial.

(Mwebantu, Thursday, 25th June, 2026)

A moderate cold wave warning is in effect for most of Zambia from Friday, 26th June to Saturday, 27th June 2026, weather officials said.

A strong high-pressure system is expected to bring increasingly windy and cool conditions nationwide, with lower temperatures, dusty conditions, reduced visibility, and hazardous gusty winds.

Cloudy conditions are forecast over Eastern and Lusaka provinces, which are the primary areas of concern.

Authorities advised the general public to keep warm and take extra precautions, especially for vulnerable groups and people with respiratory conditions.

Water transport operators were urged to suspend operations or exercise extreme caution, while farmers were told to protect sensitive crops and secure loose structures and equipment.

(Mwebantu, Thursday, 25th June, 2026)

According to LMUTH, the injury occurred during sexual activity earlier in the week. Clinical assessment showed a significant penile hematoma with injury to the corpus spongiosum and complete transection of the penile urethra. The patient also reported prior use of energy-boosting drinks.

(Mwebantu, Thursday, 25th June, 2026)

POLICE in Lusaka have detained two female juveniles aged 15 and 16 in connection with the suspected murder of 18-year-old Taonga Phiri of Kamwala South, Lusaka.

Zambia Police Public Relations Officer, Godfrey Chilabi, disclosed that the matter was reported to police on 22nd June 2026 around 20:30 hours by Michael Malambo, 40, who reported the death of his daughter under suspicious circumstances.

He added that preliminary investigations indicate that on 21 June 2026 between 08:00 and 11:00 hours, the 15-year-old juvenile now in custody contacted her mother and informed her that the deceased had collapsed at a residence in Kamwala South.

She also alerted a nearby relative who found the victim lying unconscious and unresponsive. The victim was rushed to the University Teaching Hospital where medical personnel pronounced her dead on arrival.

Investigations so far suggest the deceased may have been assaulted and strangled. It is further alleged that unknown tablets were administered after the incident in an attempt to make the death appear to be suicide.

Following further inquiries, police detained both the 15-year-old and 16-year-old juveniles in connection with the matter.

The body of the deceased has been deposited in the UTH Mortuary pending a post-mortem examination to determine the exact cause of death. The motive has not yet been established and investigations remain ongoing.

Police say they will provide further updates as investigations progress.

(Mwebantu, Wednesday, 24th June, 2026)

title: HH Urges Chavuma Voters to Back UPND for ‘More Development’
summary: PRESIDENT Hakainde Hichilema has called on residents of Chavuma to vote for the UPND in the Aug. 13 election, saying it is the only way to “protect the gains” made on peace, free education, and Constituency Development Funds, CDF.
content:
PRESIDENT Hakainde Hichilema has called on residents of Chavuma to vote for the UPND in the Aug. 13 election, saying it is the only way to “protect the gains” made on peace, free education, and Constituency Development Funds, CDF.

HH: ‘Let’s Keep Pushing Zambia Forward’

Your email address will not be published.Required fields are marked*

Save my name, email, and website in this browser for the next time I comment.

PRESIDENT Hakainde Hichilema has thanked residents of North-Western Province for a “remarkable demonstration of hope, unity and patriotism” during visits to Mwinilunga and Solwezi.

In a statement, Hichilema said thousands gathered to hear his vision for “love, peace and shared prosperity.” He reflected on 23 years in opposition and credited the province’s “loyalty, resilience and sacrifice” for sustaining the UPND through difficult times.

“From making free education a permanent right, to expanding investment in mining, agriculture, road infrastructure and other key sectors… we are proud of the progress we have made together,” he said. “Yet we remain fully conscious that our work is far from complete.”

Hichilema called North-Western Province a special place, but stressed “ours is one nation, united by a common destiny.” He urged Zambians to support the UPND in the Aug. 13, 2026 election to “protect the progress we have made, complete the work we have begun, and build an even brighter future.”

“Let’s keep pushing Zambia forward,” he said.

(Mwebantu,Thursday, 2nd July, 2026)

THE Organized People’s Party, OPP, says vice-presidential running mate Andyford Banda has stepped down, and the party has notified the Electoral Commission of Zambia, ECZ, for guidance on next legal steps.

In a statement, OPP presidential candidate Dr. Brian Mushimba said the party “fully respects” Banda’s decision and thanked him for his dedication.

“We have officially notified the Electoral Commission of Zambia and requested guidance on the next legal steps. The OPP will fully comply with the law and the directives issued by the Commission,” Mushimba said.

He added that the party “remains united, focused, and deeply committed” to its campaign promises and will update the nation once the ECZ responds.

Banda’s exit marks the first major change to the OPP’s presidential ticket ahead of next month’s election.

(Mwebantu, Wednesday, 1st July, 2026)

PRESIDENT Hakainde Hichilema has thanked Mwinilunga residents for their turnout at a meeting in Kaunda Square, saying their support shows a desire to “continue protecting the gains Zambia has made.”

In a statement posted, Hichilema said his administration’s focus is “love, care and more deliverables for all.”

“Road infrastructure remains dear to us and we are determined to work on these and many other economic fundamentals,” he said. “So much has been done but there is more to be done.”

Hichilema urged voters to support him in next month’s election, contrasting the current climate with “past times when anyone who wore a red t-shirt was living under fear of attacks from political groupings.”

“Vote Hakainde Hichilema for President. Vote for UPND,” he said, closing with “Ba Mwinilunga! Mung’wa nankashi!” — a Luvale phrase meaning “Mwinilunga people, you are very many/thank you very much.”

(Mwebantu, Wednesday, 1st July, 2026)

Zambia’s mining, energy and agriculture sectors have drawn significant investor interest as the government’s fiscal consolidation gains traction after emerging from a sovereign default, said Citi’s country chief.

“We have noted increased interest, especially when we have seen significant strides being made in terms of bringing stability to the Zambian economy,” said Lowani Chibesakunda, Citi’s chief executive and banking head for Zambia, in an interview with Reuters on Tuesday.

“We have noted key interests in mining, in energy, as well as in agriculture.”

Zambia is rich in copper, positioning it well as demand for the red metal grows, spurred by the transition to clean energy and soaring power needs for AI data centres.

“You cannot talk about the Zambian story without emphasising the importance of mining and seeing how copper prices have remained bullish,” said Chibesakunda.

“The focus that is there now on copper-related products, be it your electric vehicles, et cetera, is really driving a lot of growth in that sector.”

The government secured 97.85% participation in a cash tender offer for its $1.365 billion outstanding notes, launched on May 29, in what the Ministry of Finance described as part of broader efforts to improve debt sustainability and reduce long-term obligations. Citi acted as sole mandated bank to organize, structure, and execute the transaction.

In November, S&P Global upgraded Zambia’s long- and short-term foreign-currency credit rating to CCC+/C from SD (selective default) with a stable outlook, formally removing the country’s default status.

The upgrade followed the government’s forecast of a rosier fiscal picture for 2026, estimating the budget deficit would more than halve and targeting economic growth of more than 6% after years of protracted debt restructuring and a severe drought.

Chibesakunda said she was also seeing new categories of investors entering the market for the first time.

“We have seen increased interest from players coming through from the Middle East … just to give you a sense, the pharmaceutical sector, the technology space.” In mining, “we’ve had players coming into this market that are mining for the first time,” she said.

The broader investment surge is reflected in the data. According to United Nations trade data, Zambia attracted $1.24 billion in foreign direct investment in 2024, the highest inflow since 2015.

SOURCE: CNBC AFRICAhttps://www.cnbcafrica.com/2026/investors-have-regained-appetite-for-zambia-after-default-citi-says

content:

HH rallies Mwinilunga, cites development, peace as election nears

Mundubile, Zulu: We Will Bury Lungu if Voted Into Office

Your email address will not be published.Required fields are marked*

Save my name, email, and website in this browser for the next time I comment.

PRESIDENT Hakainde Hichilema has called on residents of Chavuma to vote for the UPND in the Aug. 13 election, saying it is the only way to “protect the gains” made on peace, free education, and Constituency Development Funds, CDF.

PRESIDENT Hakainde Hichilema has thanked residents of North-Western Province for a “remarkable demonstration of hope, unity and patriotism” during visits to Mwinilunga and Solwezi.

In a statement, Hichilema said thousands gathered to hear his vision for “love, peace and shared prosperity.” He reflected on 23 years in opposition and credited the province’s “loyalty, resilience and sacrifice” for sustaining the UPND through difficult times.

“From making free education a permanent right, to expanding investment in mining, agriculture, road infrastructure and other key sectors… we are proud of the progress we have made together,” he said. “Yet we remain fully conscious that our work is far from complete.”

Hichilema called North-Western Province a special place, but stressed “ours is one nation, united by a common destiny.” He urged Zambians to support the UPND in the Aug. 13, 2026 election to “protect the progress we have made, complete the work we have begun, and build an even brighter future.”

“Let’s keep pushing Zambia forward,” he said.

(Mwebantu,Thursday, 2nd July, 2026)

THE Organized People’s Party, OPP, says vice-presidential running mate Andyford Banda has stepped down, and the party has notified the Electoral Commission of Zambia, ECZ, for guidance on next legal steps.

In a statement, OPP presidential candidate Dr. Brian Mushimba said the party “fully respects” Banda’s decision and thanked him for his dedication.

“We have officially notified the Electoral Commission of Zambia and requested guidance on the next legal steps. The OPP will fully comply with the law and the directives issued by the Commission,” Mushimba said.

He added that the party “remains united, focused, and deeply committed” to its campaign promises and will update the nation once the ECZ responds.

Banda’s exit marks the first major change to the OPP’s presidential ticket ahead of next month’s election.

(Mwebantu, Wednesday, 1st July, 2026)

title: ZAFFICO Eyes K1.8 Billion Revenue by 2029 Through Forestry Expansion
summary: ZAMBIA Forestry and Forest Industries Corporation Plc has unveiled plans to grow annual revenue from K460 million in 2025 to K1.8 billion by 2029 as it seeks to become a fully integrated forestry and wood-processing company.
content:
ZAMBIA Forestry and Forest Industries Corporation Plc has unveiled plans to grow annual revenue from K460 million in 2025 to K1.8 billion by 2029 as it seeks to become a fully integrated forestry and wood-processing company.

Speaking at the Rights Issue Bell-Ringing Ceremony in Lusaka yesterday, Managing Director Mundia Mundia said the growth would be driven by increased investment in plantation expansion, timber processing and manufacturing infrastructure.

He said the planned rights issue would help secure future timber supply and support the company’s long-term strategy despite short-term investment costs.

Board Chairperson Dr. Alvert Ng’andu said the expansion would reduce Zambia’s reliance on imported wood products. He noted the country currently spends about US$160 million annually on particle board imports.

Industrial Development Corporation Chief Executive Officer Cornwell Muleya said the investment aligns with government’s industrialisation agenda. Lusaka Securities Exchange CEO Nicholas Kabaso described the rights issue as a positive step for Zambia’s capital markets and economic growth.

(Mwebantu, Friday, 26th June, 2026)

title: Contract talks sideline Häuptle from Brazil FIFA series
summary: COPPER Queens coach Nora Häuptle will not be part of the FIFA Series tournament in Brazil due to administrative issues.
content:
COPPER Queens coach Nora Häuptle will not be part of the FIFA Series tournament in Brazil due to administrative issues.

Football Association of Zambia (FAZ) Acting Deputy General Secretary Iva Lengwe said Häuptle has proposed amendments to her current contract, leading to prolonged negotiations that have ruled her out of the Brazil assignment.

“We regret to announce that Häuptle will not lead the Copper Queens in Brazil as a result of ongoing renegotiations for amendments to her current contract,” he said.

“Coach Nora will be travelling to Lusaka in the next few days to ensure that all outstanding issues are resolved in a professional manner. We had made all the necessary travel logistics for Coach Nora to be in Brazil, but unfortunately, she could not make it owing to her desire to secure amendments to her contract,” Lengwe said.

He said assistant coach Charles Haalubono will take charge of the Brazil assignment and will receive full support.

Meanwhile, Lengwe has wished the Copper Queens the best as they commence their FIFA Series assignment on Saturday.

“We urge the public to support the Copper Queens in their FIFA Series engagement, which we are using as a platform for preparing for WAFCON,” he said.

Lengwe also acknowledged that some players delayed travelling due to visa hitches, but all those summoned for the tournament have since departed for Brazil.

He said the original plans were hampered by injuries, and FAZ had made provisions for visa arrangements for replacements.

Zambia plays Canada on Saturday before facing Brazil on Tuesday.

The final match will be against South Korea on April 18.

This is according to a statement issued by FAZ Head of Media and Communications Nkweto Tembwe.

(Mwebantu, Friday, 10th April,2026)

Chilambe Katuta Becomes First Zambian Male to Earn Abbott Six Star Medal in Boston

Our players were slightly timid says Lwandamina

CONGOLESE striker Idris Mbombo has left Nkana Football Club after his contract expired, the Kitwe giants confirmed.

Mbombo is among five players departing Nkana ahead of the new MTN Super League season. Defender Dieugo Apanane and midfielder Joe Wallace Banda have also left following the expiry of their contracts.

Goalkeeper Patrick Chooma and midfielder Francis Zulu have returned to their parent clubs after loan spells at Nkana Stadium ended.

Club media officer Mike Mubanga confirmed the departures in a statement Saturday. “Nkana Football Club confirms that defender Dieugo Apanane, midfielder Joe Wallace Banda and striker Idris Mbombo will leave the club following the expiry of their contracts,” Mubanga said.

“The club also confirms the conclusion of the loan spells of goalkeeper Patrick Chooma and midfielder Francis Zulu, who will return to their parent club,” he added.

Mubanga said Nkana thanked all five players for their service and wished them well. The club said further updates on squad preparations for the new season would be communicated in due course.

(Mwebantu, Tuesday, 16th June, 2026)

WORLD Bicycle Day was marked in style Monday with a morning ride led by British High Commissioner to Zambia H.E. Rebecca Terzeon and Zambia’s Cultural Ambassador and “tall bicycle” icon, One YeYe International.

After the ride, the group gathered for breakfast at the British Residence in Lusaka. The conversation centered on One YeYe International’s cycling journeys across Zambia, his recent trip to Cape Town, future plans, and Zambia’s rich cultural heritage.

The moment carried extra meaning as H.E. Terzeon and One YeYe International reflected on past meetings at Zambia’s cultural ceremonies and finally completed a bike ride together. To mark the occasion, the High Commissioner presented One YeYe International with a gift celebrating their shared passion for cultural awareness and collaboration.

“Days like this remind us how diverse, rich, and fun Zambian culture is — and that sometimes, it’s best experienced on two wheels,”

ZAMBIAN engineer, amateur runner and businessman Ben Bwalya completed his 10th Sanlam Cape Town Marathon on Sunday, marking another milestone in his long-distance running career. Bwalya becomes the second Zambian to run 10 Cape Town marathons, after Ronald Mambwe, who has run 12.

The achievement places Bwalya among the event’s most consistent long-distance participants. Bwalya, proprietor of Climate Control Botswana and Climate Control Zambia, is also a Fellow of the Engineering Institution of Zambia and a recipient of an honorary doctorate and the ZABA Businessman of the Year award. He said marathon running remains a key personal passion.

“I am so grateful to my family, friends, and everyone who has been part of this journey. Every kilometre carried your support,” he said.

He said the sport continues to teach him discipline, resilience and consistency — values he also applies in his professional work.

The Sanlam Cape Town Marathon is an official Abbott World Marathon Majors candidate race. It is bidding to become the seventh World Marathon Major and the first in Africa.

(Mwebantu, Sunday, 24th May, 2026)

title: 3 Zambian Peacekeepers Wounded in Central African Republic Attack, UN Says
summary: THREE Zambian peacekeepers were injured, one seriously, after armed elements attacked Am Dafock and fired on a MINUSCA temporary base June 30, the UN mission said.
content:
THREE Zambian peacekeepers were injured, one seriously, after armed elements attacked Am Dafock and fired on a MINUSCA temporary base June 30, the UN mission said.

Cold wave warning issued for most of Zambia, including Lusaka

Your email address will not be published.Required fields are marked*

Save my name, email, and website in this browser for the next time I comment.

A moderate cold wave warning is in effect for most of Zambia from Friday, 26th June to Saturday, 27th June 2026, weather officials said.

A strong high-pressure system is expected to bring increasingly windy and cool conditions nationwide, with lower temperatures, dusty conditions, reduced visibility, and hazardous gusty winds.

Cloudy conditions are forecast over Eastern and Lusaka provinces, which are the primary areas of concern.

Authorities advised the general public to keep warm and take extra precautions, especially for vulnerable groups and people with respiratory conditions.

Water transport operators were urged to suspend operations or exercise extreme caution, while farmers were told to protect sensitive crops and secure loose structures and equipment.

(Mwebantu, Thursday, 25th June, 2026)

A 34-year-old man was treated at Levy Mwanawasa University Teaching Hospital this week for a penile fracture, a rare urological emergency requiring urgent surgical intervention, hospital officials said.

According to LMUTH, the injury occurred during sexual activity earlier in the week. Clinical assessment showed a significant penile hematoma with injury to the corpus spongiosum and complete transection of the penile urethra. The patient also reported prior use of energy-boosting drinks.

The patient was taken to theatre where urologists performed urgent surgical repair, including circumcision. The hospital said the procedure was successful and the patient is stable and recovering well.

The surgical team included urologists Dr. Muleya M, Dr. Mufaya, Dr. Lupinda C.K, and Dr. Chungu. Anaesthesia was administered by Ms. Lydia with Sr. Vivian as scrub nurse.

LMUTH said the case highlights risks associated with unregulated substances marketed for sexual enhancement, which may contribute to unsafe practices and serious health complications. The hospital urged the public to seek guidance from qualified healthcare professionals regarding sexual health concerns.

“LMUTH remains committed to delivering timely, specialized, and life-saving surgical care,” the hospital said.

(Mwebantu, Thursday, 25th June, 2026)

FILM producer and director David Kazadi, model Mwaka Halwindi, and Ruby Muzandu have pleaded not guilty to charges of producing, possessing and circulating obscene material.

The trio appeared before Lusaka Magistrates’ Court today for plea taking before Lusaka Magistrate Kawama Mwamfuli.

Mr Kazadi and Ms Halwindi denied the charges they jointly face.

Ms Muzandu also pleaded not guilty to circulating and possessing the said material.

The State alleges that between January 2023 and June 2025, Ms Muzandu circulated the alleged obscene material through a WhatsApp group known as “Trip to South Africa”.

Mr Kazadi is accused of sharing videos involving Ms Halwindi and other alleged explicit content within the same period.

After the plea, the court adjourned the matter to 24 and 25 August 2026 for commencement of trial.

(Mwebantu, Thursday, 25th June, 2026)

© Copyright © 2026 Mwebantu. All Rights Reserved.  Zambia's leading Social news platform.

title: Gospel Artist, Emmanuel JB – Son Of David, hints two new songs
summary: KNOWN for his soul-soothing music that brings comfort, solace, and reassurance to the listeners, Creative-E-Splash media signed gospel singer, songwriter and minister, Emmanuel JB, who has earned a Kindom password name tagged “Son Of David”, has hinted in an interview to the Daily Nation Lifestyl...
content:
KNOWN for his soul-soothing music that brings comfort, solace, and reassurance to the listeners, Creative-E-Splash media signed gospel singer, songwriter and minister, Emmanuel JB, who has earned a Kindom password name tagged “Son Of David”, has hinted in an interview to the Daily Nation Lifestyle that works on his soon to be released, ‘Nations’ and ‘I believe’ singles have advanced.

Inspired by renowned gospel recording artist and a lead pastor of the High Praise Centre in Vosloorus, east of Johannesburg in Benjamin Dube, a South African gospel who over the years has released several albums which have reached gold and platinum status in the music rankings and the Destined Kids, a popular child group that touched people’s hearts with melodious tunes, Emmanuel JB, who is under the leadership of Pastor Chris Chibale, of New Life Gospel Version Church, and the only child of his parents who died in the 90s said his love to do music started in his young age.

“I started having this love of music at a young age. I enjoyed listening to the Destined Kids, from Nigeria and I admired to one day be like them. But my biggest inspiration to do music came from Benjamin Dube from South Africa. I desired to sing in his team or to sing with him one day which later in time lead me to join the praise teams. In 2016, I recorded my first song called ‘Takwaba’ by Dj Tino, back in Kafue. I did few more songs with him until when I Joined E-Splash Studios with the renowned multi-gifted-producer, Eliphas, at E-Splash Studios with whom we have done a couple of good music. I can say, apart from my cousin, Adcool, who is a rapper, am the only child who took music serious to a point of recording”

The gospel singer and songwriter said works on his two upcoming songs have advanced. And when asked what message the two songs had to the listeners, Emmanuel JB responded, “The two songs talk about the possibility man born a sinner to becoming righteous as is the plan of God from the beginning of the world through the gospel of Water, Blood and the Spirit, for it shows how man became a sinner and how Christ has exchange His righteous nature with us humans. They carry with them the message of hope, faith, salvation and redemption to the listeners”

Asked what would make Zambia Gospel music reach greater hights, he said, “unremitting spiritual focus, self-discipline, sacrifice and consistency in the study of God’s Word, through holding tenaciously to Christ’s hand as He leads us. With constant commitment, Zambia will have a future Minister of God in the music industry”

title: HH: We Suffered 23 Years in Opposition, Don’t Go Back.
summary: PRESIDENT Hakainde Hichilema says UPND supporters endured 23 years of hardship in opposition and must vote to avoid returning to that era.
content:
PRESIDENT Hakainde Hichilema says UPND supporters endured 23 years of hardship in opposition and must vote to avoid returning to that era.

Speaking during a community engagement in Nkeyema District Thursday, Hichilema said party members “suffered at the hands of those who were in power.”

“When we were in opposition as UPND, we suffered. We don’t want to go back there,” he said. “If you were UPND and wore UPND regalia, when you travelled from Nkeyema or Kaoma to Lusaka Intercity, you were beaten in those days.”

He said jobs under the previous administration were “only for a few people.”

He listed his administration’s priorities as “more roads, more jobs, peace, unity and love for all,” and told residents, “Together, let’s protect the gains made this far by voting for the UPND.”

“That is where we are coming from. People were beaten very badly. In those days, if you were in opposition, you could not get jobs easily,” Hichilema said.

The President urged Nkeyema residents to “safeguard their freedom” in the Aug. 13, 2026 election.

“Today you are moving freely. You can go to Lusaka wearing your UPND cape or your UPND msisi and you are free. That is the freedom you brought about through your vote in 2021,” he said. “Do you want to go back to where you were being beaten, or do you want to remain in freedom?”

(Mwebantu, Friday, 3rd July, 2026)
